hypothesis: Applying NN-BAX to systems governed by density functional theory potentials will reveal that the optimal BAX sampling strategy should be non-uniform along the NEB path—specifically, concentrating query points near regions of high curvature in the energy landscape (i.e., near transition states and intermediate minima) rather than sampling uniformly, and that implementing this curvature-weighted acquisition will reduce the number of DFT evaluations required for convergence by an additional order of magnitude compared to the uniform random sampling demonstrated in this paper, while maintaining equivalent accuracy in energy barrier prediction.
